stem: A fair six-sided die is rolled once. What is the probability of rolling an even number?
solutionStandard: Even outcomes are 2, 4, 6: \(3\) out of \(6\), so \(P = 3/6 = 1/2\).

stem: Two fair coins are flipped. What is the probability that both show heads?
solutionStandard: The coins are independent: \(P(\text{both heads}) = \frac{1}{2} \times \frac{1}{2} = \frac{1}{4}\).

stem: A bag contains 5 red, 3 blue, and 2 green marbles. What is the probability of drawing a red marble, expressed as a percent? (Enter your answer as a number, without the percent sign.)
A card is drawn at random from a standard 52-card deck (12 face cards: jack, queen, king in each of 4 suits). What is the probability the card is a face card?
Two fair six-sided dice are rolled.
quantityA: The probability that the sum is 7
quantityB: The probability that the sum is 2
solutionStandard: Sum of 7 has 6 favorable outcomes out of 36 (\(P=6/36\)); sum of 2 has only 1 outcome (1,1), so \(P=1/36\). Since \(6/36 > 1/36\), Quantity A is greater.

stem: A bag has 4 red and 6 blue marbles, and one marble is drawn at random. Which of the following are true? Select all that apply.
solutionStandard: \(P(\text{red}) = 4/10 = 2/5\) and \(P(\text{blue}) = 6/10 = 3/5\). These are complementary events, so they must sum to 1. They are not equal to each other.

stem: Events \(A\) and \(B\) are independent, with \(P(A) = 0.4\) and \(P(B) = 0.5\). What is \(P(A \text{ and } B)\)?
solutionStandard: \(P(A \text{ and } B) = P(A) \times P(B) = 0.4 \times 0.5 = 0.2\).

stem: A jar has balls numbered 1 through 8. One ball is drawn at random. What is the probability, as a percent, that the number is greater than 5? (Enter your answer as a number, without the percent sign.)
solutionStandard: Favorable outcomes are 6, 7, 8: \(3\) out of \(8\). \(P = 3/8 = 0.375 = 37.5\%\).

stem: A committee of 2 people is chosen at random from a group of 4 men and 3 women. What is the probability that both people chosen are women?
solutionStandard: Total ways to choose 2 from 7: \(\binom{7}{2}=21\). Ways to choose 2 women from 3: \(\binom{3}{2}=3\). \(P = 3/21 = 1/7\).

stem: A fair coin is flipped 3 times. What is the probability of getting at least one head?
solutionStandard: \(P(\text{no heads}) = P(\text{all tails}) = (1/2)^3 = 1/8\). \(P(\text{at least one head}) = 1 - 1/8 = 7/8\).
solutionShortcut: "At least one" is the complement of "none." Compute the easy case (all tails) and subtract from 1, instead of adding up the cases with exactly 1, 2, or 3 heads.

stem: A fair {{n}}-sided die (faces numbered 1 to {{n}}) is rolled once. What is the probability, as a percent, that the result is even? (Enter your answer as a number, without the percent sign.)
solutionStandard: Exactly half of the numbers from 1 to {{n}} are even (since {{n}} is even), so \(P(\text{even}) = 50\%\) regardless of the die's size.

stem: Two fair coins are flipped. What is the probability that the two coins show different results (one heads, one tails)?
solutionStandard: Outcomes: HH, HT, TH, TT (each equally likely). Different results occur in HT and TH: \(2/4 = 1/2\).

stem: A bag contains 6 red, 9 blue, and 5 green marbles. What is the probability of drawing a blue marble, expressed as a percent? (Enter your answer as a number, without the percent sign.)
A card is drawn at random from a standard 52-card deck. What is the probability that the card is a spade or a heart?
solutionStandard: There are 13 spades and 13 hearts, and the two suits share no cards: \(P = 26/52 = 1/2\).

stem: Two fair six-sided dice are rolled.
quantityA: The probability that the sum is {{7-d0}}
quantityB: The probability that the sum is {{7+d0+g}}
solutionStandard: For two dice, the number of ways to make a sum \(s\) is \(6-|s-7|\). Quantity A's sum is \({{d0}}\) away from 7, while Quantity B's sum is \({{d0+g}}\) away from 7 — farther out — so Quantity B has fewer ways to occur and thus a smaller probability. Quantity A is greater.

stem: A jar has 3 red, 5 blue, and 2 yellow marbles, and one marble is drawn at random. Which of the following are true? Select all that apply.
solutionStandard: \(P(\text{red})=3/10\), \(P(\text{blue})=5/10=1/2\), \(P(\text{yellow})=2/10=1/5\). These three cover every outcome, so they must sum to 1. Red and yellow are not equal.

stem: Events A and B are independent, with \(P(A) = {{pA/10}}\) and \(P(B) = {{pB/10}}\). What is \(P(A \text{ and } B)\)?
solutionStandard: \(P(A \text{ and } B) = P(A) \times P(B) = {{pA/10}} \times {{pB/10}} = {{Math.round((pA/10)*(pB/10)*100)/100}}\).

stem: A jar has balls numbered 1 through 8. One ball is drawn at random. What is the probability, as a percent, that the number is greater than {{8-f}}? (Enter your answer as a number, without the percent sign.)
solutionStandard: Favorable outcomes are the {{f}} numbers greater than {{8-f}}, out of 8: \(P = {{f}}/8 = {{100*f/8}}\%\).

stem: A committee of 2 people is chosen at random from a group of 5 men and 4 women. What is the probability that both people chosen are men?
solutionStandard: Total ways to choose 2 from 9: \(\binom{9}{2}=36\). Ways to choose 2 men from 5: \(\binom{5}{2}=10\). \(P = 10/36 = 5/18\).

stem: A fair coin is flipped {{n}} times. What is the probability of getting at least one head, as a percent? (Enter your answer as a number, without the percent sign.)
solutionStandard: \(P(\text{no heads})\) means every flip lands tails: multiply \(1/2\) by itself {{n}} times. \(P(\text{at least one head}) = 100\% - P(\text{no heads}) = {{100*(1-1/(2**n))}}\%\).
